2603.23300.title: Designing Agentic AI-Based Screening for Portfolio Investment
2603.23300.paper_summary: We introduce a new agentic artificial intelligence (AI) platform for portfolio management. Our architecture consists of three layers. First, two large language model (LLM) agents are assigned specialized tasks: one agent screens for firms with desirable fundamentals, while a sentiment analysis agent screens for firms with desirable news. Second, these agents deliberate to generate and agree upon buy and sell signals from a large portfolio, substantially narrowing the pool of candidate assets. Finally, we apply a high-dimensional precision matrix estimation procedure to determine optimal portfolio weights. A defining theoretical feature of our framework is that the number of assets in the portfolio is itself a random variable, realized through the screening process. We introduce the concept of sensible screening and establish that, under mild screening errors, the squared Sharpe ratio of the screened portfolio consistently estimates its target. Empirically, our method achieves superior Sharpe ratios relative to an unscreened baseline portfolio and to conventional screening approaches, evaluated on S&P 500 data over the period 2020--2024.
2603.23300.paper_summary_zh: 我們介紹了一個新的代理人工智慧（AI）平台，用於投資組合管理。<lb/>我們的架構由三個層次組成。<lb/>首先，兩個大型語言模型（LLM）代理被分配專門任務：一個代理負責篩選具有理想基本面的公司，而情感分析代理則篩選具有理想新聞的公司。<lb/>其次，這些代理進行討論，以生成並達成對大型投資組合的買入和賣出信號，從而大幅縮小候選資產的範圍。<lb/>最後，我們應用高維精確矩陣估計程序來確定最佳投資組合權重。<lb/>我們框架的一個定義性理論特徵是，投資組合中的資產數量本身是一個隨機變數，通過篩選過程實現。<lb/>我們引入了合理篩選的概念，並確立在輕微篩選錯誤下，篩選後投資組合的平方夏普比率始終能夠一致地估計其目標。<lb/>在實證方面，我們的方法相較於未篩選的基準投資組合和傳統篩選方法，在2020年至2024年期間的標準普爾500數據上達到了更優的夏普比率。
2603.23300.author: Mehmet Caner et.al.
2603.23300.authors: Mehmet Caner, Agostino Capponi, Nathan Sun, Jonathan Y. Tan
